evals.prompt: I want to run a dynamic security scan on our staging API before the release. What tools should I use and how?
evals.expected_output: Use Nuclei for fast CVE/misconfiguration scanning, ZAP-CLI for deep XSS/SQLi scanning. Set max-depth and max-duration to prevent infinite loops. Use authenticated headers (Authorization) to test protected surfaces. Never scan production — staging only.

evals.prompt: We don't have ZAP or Nuclei installed. How can I still do adversarial testing on our API endpoints?
evals.expected_output: Use AI-driven curl probes: (1) Bypass guards with manipulated headers like X-Forwarded-For. (2) Probe /metrics, /health, /.git for data leakage. (3) Tamper parameters — inject objects where strings expected, send oversized payloads. Document findings with severity scoring.

evals.prompt: Our DAST scan found the server exposes its version string in response headers. How severe is this?
evals.expected_output: Severity P1 — Info Leakage (Server version/Env vars): -10 deduction. Attackers can target known CVEs for that version. Fix: configure web server to suppress version headers (e.g., server_tokens off in Nginx). Also check for X-Powered-By, X-AspNet-Version headers.

should_not_trigger: Write a REST API endpoint in Express.js
How do I optimize my PostgreSQL queries?
Set up Tailwind CSS in a Next.js project
